diagrams.description: Macroeconomics aims to achieve three ultimate goals: sustainable economic growth, full employment, and price stability. Progress is measured by monitoring key aggregates – GDP, unemployment, inflation, and the balance of payments. These indicators guide the choice of fiscal and monetary policy tools, while analytical models such as AD‑AS, IS‑LM, and the Phillips Curve help explain the relationships between variables and validate policy actions. The flowchart captures this cyclical process: from goals to measurement, to policy intervention, and back to the goals.
